Name: Oddish
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

Name: Gloom
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

Name: Bellossom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Bellossom gather at times and appear to dance. They say that the dance is a ritual to summon the sun.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

Name: Shuckle
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They usually live on ponds, but after an evening shower, they may appear on puddles in towns.

Name: Masquerain
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction—to and fro and sideways.

Name: Combee
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

Name: Foongus
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [G] Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: There is a theory that the developer of the modern-day Poké Ball really liked Foongus, but this has not been confirmed.

Name: Amoonguss
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Be wary of the poisonous spores it releases. Mushrooms resembling Amoonguss’s caps will grow out of anywhere the spores touch.

Name: Phantump
Flavor-Text: With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

Name: Trevenant
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon’s feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.

Name: Rowlet
Flavor-Text: It feels relaxed in tight, dark places and has been known to use its Trainer’s pocket or bag as a nest.

Name: Dartrix
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Supremely sensitive to the presence of others, it can detect opponents standing behind it, flinging its sharp feathers to take them out.

Name: Decidueye ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. If this Pokémon is on the Bench, switch it with your Active Pokémon. Or, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bounsweet
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled- down fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in the past, when sweeteners were scarce.

Name: Steenee
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Steenee spreads a sweet scent that makes others feel invigorated. This same scent is popular for antiperspirants.

Name: Tsareena
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, Pokémon can’t be played from your opponent’s hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.

Name: Smoliv
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

Name: Dolliv
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

Name: Arboliva
Effects: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

Name: Toedscruel ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Capsakid
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.

Name: Capsakid
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.

Name: Scovillain
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [G] and [R] type.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.

Name: Charmander
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Put 9 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

Name: Entei
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Numel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Magma of almost 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit courses through its body. When it grows cold, the magma hardens and slows it.

Name: Camerupt
Effects: Discard the top card of each player’s deck. This attack does 100 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the crater of a volcano. It is well known that the humps on its back erupt every 10 years.

Name: Victini ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darumaka
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The younger the life this Pokémon absorbs, the brighter and eerier the flame on its head burns.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lurks in cities, pretending to be a lamp. Once it finds someone whose death is near, it will trail quietly after them.

Name: Chandelure
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of lights, funerals were a constant occurrence— or so it’s said.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

Name: Larvesta
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was called the Larva That Stole the Sun. The fire Larvesta spouts from its horns can cut right through a sheet of iron.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Attach a Basic [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its burning body causes it to be unpopular in hot parts of the world, but in cold ones, Volcarona is revered as an embodiment of the sun.

Name: Eiscue ex
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Armarouge
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

Name: Lapras
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.

Name: Sharpedo
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is known as the Bully of the Sea. Any ship entering the waters Sharpedo calls home will be attacked—no exceptions.

Name: Buizel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put your opponent’s Active Pokémon and all attached cards into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

Name: Tympole
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can’t hear its cries of warning.

Name: Palpitoad
Flavor-Text: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

Name: Seismitoad
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon cost [C] more.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.

Name: Cubchoo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Many of this species can be found along the shorelines of cold regions. If a Cubchoo lacks dangling snot, there’s a chance it is sick.

Name: Beartic
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is a ferocious, carnivorous Pokémon. Once it captures its prey, it will breathe cold air onto the prey to freeze and preserve it.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: If you go second and it’s your first turn, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Cryogonal appear during cold seasons. It is said that people and Pokémon who die on snowy mountains are reborn into these Pokémon.

Name: Froakie
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Frogadier
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

Name: Wiglett
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

Name: Wugtrio
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

Name: Finizen
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

Name: Finizen
Flavor-Text: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

Name: Palafin
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon didn’t move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Flavor-Text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Magnezone
Effects: You may switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

Name: Tyranitar ex
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Tynamo
Flavor-Text: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

Name: Eelektrik
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, this attack does 60 damage to the new Active Pokémon, and then flip a coin. If heads, that Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

Name: Thundurus
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: As it flies around, it shoots lightning all over the place and causes forest fires. It is therefore disliked.

Name: Toxel
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel’s body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Many youths admire the way this Pokémon listlessly picks fights and keeps its cool no matter what opponent it faces.

Name: Pawmot ex
Effects: Discard 2 [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 220 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tadbulb
Effects: Attach a Basic [L] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

Name: Tadbulb
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

Name: Tadbulb
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

Name: Bellibolt
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

Name: Bellibolt
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [L] Pokémon.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

Name: Miraidon ex
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Attach a Basic [L] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cleffa
Effects: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Because of its unusual, starlike silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its adorable behavior and cry make it highly popular. However, this cute Pokémon is rarely found.

Name: Clefable ex
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have [P] Energy attached have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: You may move any amount of [P] Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Togepi
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It is considered to be a symbol of good luck. Its shell is said to be filled with happiness.

Name: Togetic
Effects: Each player draws 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: It grows dispirited if it is not with kind people. It can float in midair without moving its wings.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: Once at the end of your turn (after your attack), you may use this Ability. Draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as a bringer of blessings, it’s been depicted on good-luck charms since ancient times.

Name: Espeon
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The tip of its forked tail quivers when it is predicting its opponent’s next move.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: In contrast to its appearance, it’s quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

Name: Granbull
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it’s totally incompetent as a watchdog.

Name: Mawile
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Spoink
Flavor-Text: Spoink will die if it stops bouncing. The pearl on its head amplifies its psychic powers.

Name: Grumpig
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

Name: Lunatone
Ability-Effect: If you have Solrock in play, prevent all effects of any Stadium done to your Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

Name: Solrock
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

Name: Claydol
Effects: Put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10. If you placed any damage counters in this way, this attack also does 120 damage to this Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

Name: Vespiquen ex
Effects: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Sinistea
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The soul of someone who died alone possessed some leftover tea. This Pokémon appears in hotels and houses.

Name: Polteageist
Effects: Put up to 2 in any combination of Item cards and Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Put 5 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The tea that composes Polteageist’s body has a distinct and enjoyable flavor. Drinking too much, however, can be fatal.

Name: Greavard
Flavor-Text: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

Name: Greavard
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 2 or 4 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

Name: Houndstone ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
This attack does 10 more damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Diglett
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

Name: Larvitar
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

Name: Nosepass
Effects: Put up to 2 Basic [M] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close.

Name: Barboach
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey’s location.

Name: Whiscash
Effects: For each [F] Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely protective of its territory. If any foe approaches, it attacks using vicious tremors.

Name: Bonsly
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: In order to adjust the level of fluids in its body, it exudes water from its eyes. This makes it appear to be crying.

Name: Drilbur
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.

Name: Stunfisk
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, has a Pokémon Tool attached, and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 5 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: For some reason, this Pokémon smiles slightly when it emits a strong electric current from the yellow markings on its body.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

Name: Crabrawler
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon punches trees and eats the berries that drop down, training itself and getting food at the same time.

Name: Crabominable
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: The detached pincers of these Pokémon are delicious. Some Trainers bring Lechonk into the mountains just to search for them.

Name: Rockruff
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.

Name: Toedscool
Flavor-Text: Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.

Name: Toedscruel
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

Name: Klawf ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), discard an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Glimmet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.

Name: Glimmet
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.

Name: Glimmora ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t have more than 3 Benched Pokémon. If they have 4 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 3 Pokémon on the Bench. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Koraidon ex
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic [R] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Paldean Wooper
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Flavor-Text: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

Name: Paldean Clodsire
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It’s a risky move that puts everything on the line.

Name: Paldean Clodsire
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It’s a risky move that puts everything on the line.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on that Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

Name: Houndour
Effects: For each of your Benched Houndour, search your deck for a Basic [D] Energy card and attach it to that Houndour. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 70 more damage.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

Name: Houndoom ex
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Absol ex
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of either player’s deck and put them back in any order.
If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Inkay
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee.

Name: Malamar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your opponent’s deck. If you do, look at the top card of your deck.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that Malamar’s hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events.

Name: Salandit
Flavor-Text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

Name: Salazzle
Flavor-Text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

Name: Scizor
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play that has an Ability.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object they get ahold of into bits.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slashing Steel.
Flavor-Text: People fashion swords from Skarmory’s shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.

Name: Mawile
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It uses three small units to catch prey and battle enemies. The main body mostly just gives orders.

Name: Excadrill
Flavor-Text: Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

Name: Kingambit
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has 4 or more damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

Name: Togedemaru
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: When it’s in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: They live as a group, but when the time comes, one strong Meltan will absorb all the others and evolve.

Name: Melmetal ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [M] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Varoom
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Varoom
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Revavroom ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 4 Pokémon Tools attached to it. If it loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tools from it until only 1 remains.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dratini
Flavor-Text: It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

Name: Dragonite ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 140 more damage. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Altaria
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.
Flavor-Text: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.

Name: Drampa
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it’s angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

Name: Pidgey
Flavor-Text: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

Name: Pidgeotto
Flavor-Text: Very protective of its sprawling territorial area, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

Name: Pidgeot ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Quick Search Ability each turn.
Effects: You may discard a Stadium in play.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Zigzagoon
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

Name: Linoone
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.

Name: Swablu
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Lillipup
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is far brighter than the average child, and Lillipup won’t forget the love it receives or any abuse it suffers.

Name: Herdier
Flavor-Text: The black fur that covers this Pokémon’s body is dense and springy. Even sharp fangs bounce right off.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Stoutland is immensely proud of its impressive moustache. It’s said that moustache length is what determines social standing among this species.

Name: Audino
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers, Audino can gauge other creatures’ feelings and physical conditions.

Name: Bouffalant
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon live in herds of about 20 individuals. Bouffalant that betray the herd will lose the hair on their heads for some reason.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

Name: Yungoos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach fills most of its torso. It wanders the same path every day, searching for fresh food.

Name: Gumshoos
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

Name: Skwovet
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It stores berries in its cheeks. When there are no berries to be found, Skwovet will stuff pebbles into its cheeks to stave off its cravings.

Name: Greedent ex
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may put those cards into your hand. If you don’t, discard those cards and draw 3 cards.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slip ‘n’ Roll.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Lechonk
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Lechonk
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Lechonk
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Oinkologne
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

Name: Oinkologne
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon sends a flowerlike scent wafting about. Well-developed muscles in its legs allow it to leap more than 16 feet with no trouble at all.

Name: Flamigo
Effects: If Flamigo is on your Bench, this attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.

Name: Arven
Description: Search your deck for an Item card and a Pokémon Tool card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Brassius
Description: Count the cards in your hand, shuffle those cards into your deck, then draw that many cards plus 1.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Geeta
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. During this turn, your Pokémon can’t attack. (This includes Pokémon that come into play this turn.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Letter of Encouragement
Description: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Ortega
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck. If you put a card on the bottom of your opponent’s deck in this way, your opponent may draw a card.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Patrol Cap
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, cards in your deck can’t be discarded by effects of your opponent’s attacks, Abilities, Item cards, Pokémon Tool cards, or Supporter cards.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Pokémon League Headquarters
Description: Attacks used by each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) cost [C] more.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Poppy
Description: Move up to 2 Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Ryme
Description: Draw 3 cards. Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Team Star Grunt
Description: Put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon on top of their deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Town Store
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Vengeful Punch
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, put 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Gloom
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Put 9 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

Name: Palafin
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon didn’t move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

Name: Bellibolt
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [L] Pokémon.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

Name: Cleffa
Effects: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Because of its unusual, starlike silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Larvitar
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

Name: Houndour
Effects: For each of your Benched Houndour, search your deck for a Basic [D] Energy card and attach it to that Houndour. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play that has an Ability.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object they get ahold of into bits.

Name: Pidgeotto

Name: Tyranitar ex
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Vespiquen ex
Effects: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Glimmora ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t have more than 3 Benched Pokémon. If they have 4 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 3 Pokémon on the Bench. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
